description: Implement the unified JobIngestionNode that processes pending job tracker entries from URL and local file sources, normalizes each source into parse-ready text, converts that text into strict JobDetailsSchema objects, generates canonical job IDs when needed, and records ingestion failures without blocking the rest of the batch.

userStories.title: Normalize URL and File Job Sources for Ingestion
userStories.description: As a strategic job seeker, I want the system to ingest job postings from either URLs or local files so that I can process applications even when a job board blocks direct scraping.
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario: URL source is converted into normalized job text
Given a pending job tracker entry with source_type set to url and a valid source URL
When JobIngestionNode processes the entry
Then it calls the existing stealth scraper utility and HTML parser to produce normalized job text for downstream parsing
Scenario: File source is converted into normalized job text
Given a pending job tracker entry with source_type set to file and a valid local text or HTML file path
When JobIngestionNode processes the entry
Then it reads the file contents and normalizes them into parse-ready job text using the existing extraction path where applicable
Scenario: Existing scraped jobs are preserved
Given ParentGraphState already contains scraped_jobs
When JobIngestionNode is invoked again
Then it returns the existing scraped_jobs without duplicating or reprocessing pending jobs
Relevant pytest coverage passes

tasks.description: Review `src/nodes/parent_nodes.py` and split source loading/normalization into small helper functions without changing the public `job_ingestion` entry point.
Ensure `source_type == "url"` uses the existing `src.utils.scraper.fetch_url_stealth` utility and always quits the webdriver after the fetch attempt.
Ensure `source_type == "file"` reads the local file path using UTF-8 and supports both raw text and HTML-like content without requiring network access.
Apply `src.utils.html_parser.extract_job_details` consistently so both URL and file inputs produce clean parse-ready text.
Update or add unit tests in `tests/test_parent_nodes.py` for URL, file, and idempotent scraped_jobs behavior.

userStories.notes: This story should preserve the F-04 scraper/parser integration rather than replacing it. Keep external browser and network behavior mocked in tests.

userStories.title: Parse Normalized Job Text into JobDetailsSchema
userStories.description: As a strategic job seeker, I want each ingested posting converted into the strict JobDetailsSchema so that downstream fit evaluation can rely on consistent structured data regardless of the original source.
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario: Normalized text is parsed into strict job details
Given normalized job posting text and the original JobTrackerEntry metadata
When JobIngestionNode parses the posting
Then it returns a JobDetailsSchema containing job_title, company, location, job_id, category, salary_range, requirements, nice_to_haves, responsibilities, and raw_text
Scenario: Missing optional fields do not break ingestion
Given normalized job posting text that does not include salary or nice-to-have requirements
When the posting is parsed
Then salary_range may be null, nice_to_haves may be empty, and required schema fields are still populated safely
Scenario: Parsed output is schema validated
Given parser output with malformed field types
When JobIngestionNode attempts to construct JobDetailsSchema
Then the job is not added to scraped_jobs and is recorded in failed_jobs
Relevant pytest coverage passes

tasks.description: Create a focused parsing helper in the parent ingestion module, or a new utility module if needed, that accepts normalized text plus JobTrackerEntry and returns JobDetailsSchema.
Implement deterministic field population from JobTrackerEntry for job_id, category, title fallback, and company fallback before invoking any LLM-specific parsing path.
Add an injectable parsing boundary so tests can mock structured LLM parsing without making real OpenAI calls.
Validate final parsed results by constructing `JobDetailsSchema` and ensure `raw_text` always preserves the normalized text used for parsing.
Add tests covering successful structured parsing, missing optional fields, malformed parser output, and no-network mocked parsing behavior.

userStories.notes: Do not perform real LLM/API calls in unit tests. If an LLM dependency is introduced, use the project's `uv` workflow and keep the parser boundary mockable.

userStories.title: Generate Canonical Job IDs During Ingestion
userStories.description: As a strategic job seeker, I want each parsed job to receive a consistent canonical job ID so that generated outputs, checkpoints, and tracker entries can be traced back to the correct application.
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario: Missing or placeholder job ID is generated canonically
Given a pending job whose job_id is empty, missing, or a placeholder value
When JobIngestionNode successfully identifies the company name during ingestion
Then it assigns a job_id using the MM-DD_n_company_name format with a stable batch index
Scenario: Existing valid job ID is preserved
Given a pending job with an existing valid job_id
When JobIngestionNode processes the job
Then the existing job_id is preserved in the resulting JobDetailsSchema
Scenario: Company name is sanitized for file-safe IDs
Given a company name containing spaces or punctuation
When a canonical job_id is generated
Then spaces are converted to hyphen separators and unsafe filename characters are removed
Relevant pytest coverage passes

tasks.description: Implement a helper that determines whether an existing job_id is valid or should be regenerated.
Implement canonical ID generation using the current date, one-based batch index, and sanitized company name in the `MM-DD_n_company_name` format.
Ensure company fallback order is parsed company, tracker company, then a safe unknown-company placeholder if neither is available.
Thread canonical job_id generation into JobDetailsSchema creation without mutating unrelated tracker fields.
Add tests for valid ID preservation, placeholder replacement, date/index formatting, and company-name sanitization.

userStories.notes: The TRD requires canonical IDs for downstream output naming. Keep generated IDs deterministic in tests by patching the date source.

userStories.title: Record Ingestion Failures Without Blocking the Batch
userStories.description: As a strategic job seeker, I want failed or blocked job postings to be isolated from successful postings so that one bad source does not stop the rest of my application batch from being processed.
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario: Scraper failure is recorded as a failed job
Given a URL job source that raises a scraper exception or anti-bot failure
When JobIngestionNode processes the batch
Then the job is appended to failed_jobs and remaining pending jobs continue processing
Scenario: Unsupported source type is recorded as a failed job
Given a pending job with an unsupported source_type
When JobIngestionNode processes the batch
Then the job is appended to failed_jobs and no JobDetailsSchema is emitted for it
Scenario: Missing local file is recorded as a failed job
Given a file source path that does not exist
When JobIngestionNode attempts to read it
Then the job is appended to failed_jobs and the batch continues
Relevant pytest coverage passes

tasks.description: Make JobIngestionNode catch source-loading, normalization, parsing, and schema-validation failures per job rather than around the entire batch.
Ensure failed jobs are returned in `failed_jobs` while successful jobs in the same batch still appear in `scraped_jobs`.
When appropriate, set failed URL job status to `MANUAL_INPUT_REQUIRED` on the failed JobTrackerEntry copy so the future UI can request pasted raw text.
Avoid infinite retries or repeated browser creation after a single source has failed in the current invocation.
Add tests for scraper exceptions, unsupported source types, missing files, and mixed success/failure batches.

userStories.notes: This story prepares the later Streamlit HITL fallback while keeping F-05 focused on ingestion state outputs only.
